ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: And God blessed the day after the rain and made it holy: because it was on it that He rested from all the work He had done. Eden Ture

text: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.
translated: You are in the dust and you will surely return there; because it was from there you were taken: for you are dust, and to dust you will return.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Na homara nyim dɛ m’ahoɔdzen nyinara na medze asom hom egya.
translated: And you should know that all my strength I have used to serve you, father.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the Bethel God, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me; now arise, leave this land, and return to your native land.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob was angry, and he argued with Laban: and Jacob answered Laban, saying, What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have pursued me so fiercely?

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac had not been with me, surely by now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my hardship and the labor of my hands, and last night he rebuked you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Na Jakob faa ɔbo, na ɔdze yɛɛ idum sii hɔ.
translated: And Jacob took a stone, and used it as a pillow there.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu abɔfo ma wodzii n’enyim kan kɔr no nua Esau hɔ wɔ Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ɔno se dɛ, W’akowaa Jakob dze a; ɔyɛ akyɛdze a wɔdze rekɛma mo wura Esau: na, hwɛ, ɔnoara so dzi hɛn ekyir.
translated: He said, "It is your servant Jacob who has sent them; he is a gift being given to my lord Esau. And look, he himself is behind us."

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but rather, Israel: because you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe behyiaa no.
translated: When Jacob was on his way, God's angels met him.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: When Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp." So he named that place Mahanaim.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na ɔkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper wɔ hɔ ara ma okodur adzekyee.
translated: He spoke only to Jacob; and some men with him spent the night there and left early in the morning.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, They will no longer call your name Jacob, but rather, Israel: because you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;
translated: Then Jacob was very afraid and distressed. He divided the people who were with him, as well as the sheep, goats, cattle, and camels, into two groups;

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Nna ma memfa nyimpa a wɔka mo ho yi bi mingya wo. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ma ɔnyɛ dɛn? Wɔmma minnya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.
translated: And Esau said, Please do not take some of these people who are with you away from me. And he said, What should I do? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Jakob sii mu kɔr Sukɔt, na okesii dan maa noho, na osisii mpata maa no mbowa: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Sukɔt.
translated: And Jacob continued on to Succoth, and he built a house for himself, and made shelters for his livestock; that is why the place was called Succoth.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asaase kakraba a ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ do no, wɔ Sekem n’egya Hamor ne mba no hɔ, maa dwetɛ ɔha.
translated: And he bought the small piece of land where he had pitched his tent, from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for a hundred pieces of silver.

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Mbarimba yi wɔnye hɛn tse no asomdwee mu; dɛm ntsi hom mma wɔntsena asaase yi do, na wondzi mu gua; na, hwɛ asaase yi dɔɔ so ara yie ma hɔn; hom mma yɛmfa hɔn mbabaa nwar hɔn, na wɔmma hɛn so yɛmfa hɛn mbabaa mma hɔn.
translated: These strangers are living among us in peace; therefore, let them remain on this land and not be driven away; and, see to it that they are well taken care of here; let us not take their daughters from them, and let them also not take our daughters from us.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Wɔfaa hɔn nguan nye hɔn anantwi, nye hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ nye dza ɔwɔ kurow no mu, na dza ɔwɔ haban mu;
translated: They took their sheep and their cattle, and their donkeys and whatever was in the town, and whatever was in the fields;

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ne kra rifi no mu no , ɔtoo no dzin Ben-oni; na n’egya dze ɔfrɛɛ no Benjamin.
translated: But as she was dying, she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: na Oholibama woo Jeus, Jalam, nye Kora: woyinom nye Esau ne mba a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ Keenan asaase do no.
translated: And Oholibamah gave birth to Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these are the children of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Woyinom nye Esau a ɔnoara nye Edom no n’asefo, na woyinom nye hɔn ekunyin. Seir Asefo (
translated: [These are the descendants of Esau, who is Edom, and these are their chiefs. The Chiefs of Seir (

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Na woyinom nye Dison ne mba; Hemdan, Esban, Itran nye Keran.
translated: And these are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Keran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,
translated: The chiefs who came out of Hor are these; the chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Na no nuanom enyi beer no do; na n’egya dze ɔdze asɛm no too ne tsirmu. Israel Soma Josef Kɔ No Nuanom Nkyɛn
translated: His brothers did not like him; and his father kept the matter in his mind. Israel sent Joseph to his brothers.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Na wɔsesee hɔnho dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔdaasofo no na ɔreba no.
translated: And they said, Look, the messenger is coming.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Na Juda dze apɔnkyeba no maa ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi no, dɛ ɔmfa nkɛgye ahobadze no wɔ ɔbaa no nsa mu: naaso oennhu no.
translated: So Judah gave the young goat to his friend the Adullamite, to take it and retrieve the pledge from the woman's hand; but he did not find her.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔrowo no, nna kor dze ne nsa puei; na awogyefo no faa ahoma memen dze kyekyeer ne nsa dɛ, Oyi na odzii kan puei.
translated: And it happened that when she was giving birth, a hand came out first; and the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his hand, saying, This one came out first.

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, daa daa nyinara ɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef, naaso ɔanngye no pen, dɛ ɔnye no bɛda, anaa ɔnye no bɛyɛ biribi.
translated: But the woman always spoke to Joseph, yet he did not agree to sleep with her or do anything with her.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Na hɔn beenu nyina sosoo daa, anafua da kor a ɔsor obiara ne daa, nye ne nkyerɛasee.
translated: And both of them always changed places, each night one would be above the other, and that is its meaning.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: na Farao no kɔɔpow kitsa me nsamu, na metsetseew wenyinamba no, na mikyii guu Farao no kɔɔpow mu, na medze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa.
translated: And Pharaoh went to the riverbank early in the morning, and I stood by the riverbank, and I greeted Pharaoh at the riverbank, and I gave the riverbank into Pharaoh's hand.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na sɛ inya beebi si a, ɔno kaa me, na yɛ me ayamuyie, meserɛ wo, na bɔ me dzin kyerɛ Farao, na beyi me fi dan yi mu:
translated: But if you get a place to stay, remember me, and do me this kindness: please mention me to Pharaoh, and get me out of this prison.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Na Josef buae na ɔsee dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi: nkɛntsɛn ebiasa no yɛ ndaansa:
translated: And Joseph answered and said, Its meaning is this: the three baskets are three days:

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Na Nyankopɔn hun kan no dɛ oye: na Nyankopɔn tseew kan no fii sum no ho.
translated: And God’s spirit was good: and God’s first light came out of the darkness.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning, the second day.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebiasa nye no.
translated: What goes around comes around, the day you throw a stone is not the day it returns to you.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.
translated: When God created humans in his own image, yes, in God's image he created humans; he created them male and female.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, mama hom nduadzewa na nduaba a ɔwɔ asaase nyinara enyi, dɛ ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom:
translated: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for food:

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.
translated: Heaven and earth and everything in them were finished.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa da a otsia esuon no do na ɔtseew ho: osiandɛ ɔno mu na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma a ɔayɛ nyinara ho. Eden Ture
translated: And God blessed the day He rested from His work and set it apart: because in Him, He rested from all the work He had done. Eden Ture

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn faa nyimpa no, na ɔdze no kɔtoo Eden ture no mu dɛ onsiesie hɔ, na ɔnhwɛ hɔ.
translated: And the LORD God took the man and put him in the Garden of Eden to tend it and to keep it.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no dze, mma nndzi bi: na da ara a ibedzi bi no, ampaara dɛ ibowu.
translated: If a father uses the stick of wisdom for discipline, children do not eat any; and the very day a child eats some, surely that child will die.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn maa nda heii tɔɔ nyimpa no do, na ɔdae; na oyii ne mparba mu kor, na ɔdze nam hyɛɛ n’ananmu.
translated: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: osiandɛ Nyankopɔn nyim dɛ da a hom bedzi bi no, hom enyi bobue, na hom ayɛ dɛ Nyankopɔn na hom ehu papa nye bɔn.
translated: Because God knows that the day you will eat it, your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn bisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Na ɔbaa no see dɛ, Abowatsena no na ɔdaadaa me ma midzii.
translated: And the LORD God asked the woman, What is this that you have done? And the woman said, The serpent deceived me and I ate.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Medze ɔtan mobɔto ɔwo nye ɔbaa no ntamu, na w’asefo nye n’asefo ntamu: ɔbɔbɔ wo tsir, na wo so asɔw n’anantsir.
translated: With hatred, I strike between you and the woman, and between your descendants and her descendants: he will bruise your head, and you will bruise his heel.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.
translated: You are dust and you will return to the ground; for from it you were taken: and you are dust, and to dust you will return.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nwoma ntar maa Adam nye ne yer, na ɔdze hyɛɛ hɔn. Wɔpam Adam na Eve fi Ture no mu
translated: And the LORD God made garments of cloth for Adam and his wife, and He clothed them. Adam and Eve were driven out of the Garden.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: na Abel so dze no nguan mu mbakan na dza wɔadodɔ seradze no mu bi bae. Na EWURADZE n’enyi sɔɔ Abel nye n’afɔrbɔ:
translated: And Abel also brought some of the firstborn of his flock and some of their fat portions. And the LORD was pleased with Abel and his offering:

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: na Kain nye n’afɔrbɔ dze n’enyi annsɔ. Na Kain no bo fuuw kɛsenara, na omunae.
translated: Kain did not accept his offering. Kain became very angry, and he frowned.

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Na Kain see EWURADZE dɛ, Mo bɔn ho akatua so kyɛn dɛ mubotum asoa.
translated: And Cain said to the LORD, "My punishment is greater than I can bear."

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Na Kain nye ne yer dae: na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo Enok: na ɔkyekyeer kurow, na ɔfrɛɛ kurow no ne dzin Enok, dɛ mbrɛ ne ba no ne dzin tse.
translated: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bore Enoch; and he built a city, and called the name of the city after the name of his son, Enoch.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Na Enok woo Irad; na Irad woo Mehujael: na Mehujael woo Metusael: na Metusael woo Lamek.
translated: And Enoch begot Irad; and Irad begot Mehujael; and Mehujael begot Methusael; and Methusael begot Lamech.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Na Zila, so woo Tubal-kain a ɔton kɔɔber nye dadze ndzɛmba a wɔdze twa adze nyinara no; na Tubal-kain n’akyerɛbaa nye Naama.
translated: And Zillah, she bore Tubal-cain, who forged all kinds of tools out of bronze and iron; and Tubal-cain’s sister was Naamah.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Na Set so, wɔwoo babanyin maa no; na ɔtoo no dzin Enos: nna afei adasa hyɛɛ ase bɔɔ EWURADZE ne dzin sɔɔr no.
translated: And Seth also had a son; and he named him Enos: then people began to call on the name of the LORD.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Kenan ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye du: na owui.
translated: And Kenan lived nine hundred and ten years: and he died.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Mahalalel ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaawɔtwe eduokron enum.
translated: Mahalalel lived for eight hundred and ninety-five years.

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Nda no mu no nna eso n’ekyir no, nna abran akɛse wɔ asaase do, osiandɛ ber a Nyankopɔn ne mba nye nyimpa mbabaa hyiae no, wɔwoo mba maa hɔn, woyinom nye tsetse etumfo no, nkorɔfo a wɔgyee dzin no.
translated: In those days and after that time, there were giants on the earth, because when God’s sons came to the daughters of men and had children with them, these children became the mighty men of old, people of renown.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.
translated: The Lord saw that He had made man on earth, and it grieved Him in His heart.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Iyinom nye Noa no ho asɛm. Nna Noa yɛ nyimpa tseneneenyi na odzi mu wɔ n’ebusuasantsen mu: Noa nye Nyankopɔn nantsewee.
translated: These are the stories about Noah. At that time, Noah was a righteous man and lived among his people: Noah walked with God.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara fa hɔn mu esuon esuon, onyin nye ne ber; na mbowa a hɔn ho nntsew no mu ebien ebien, onyin na ne ber;
translated: All animals with smooth skins are generally slippery, young and in their prime; but among animals with rough skins, some are young and in their prime.

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: bun no no nsunyiwa so nye ɔsor ntokura ano sisiwii, na nsu a ofi sor gyaa tɔ;
translated: He did not pour the wine on the ground as an offering to the heavens, and the water that came from above stopped falling.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Na ehɛn no kesii Ararat nkokwaa do wɔ bosoom a otsia esuon no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia duesuon no mu.
translated: And the ark rested upon the mountains of Ararat, in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: na ebubur no ennya ne nan sibew, na ɔsanee baa no hɔ wɔ hɛn no mu, osiandɛ nsu no wɔ asaase nyinara enyi: na ɔtseen ne nsa suoo no mu, na ɔdze no baa ne nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu.
translated: But the dove did not find a place to rest its feet, so it returned to him in the ark, because there was water over the whole surface of the earth. He reached out his hand, took the dove, and brought it back to himself in the ark.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Na EWURADZE tsee afɔr no no huam; na EWURADZE kaa wɔ n’akoma mu dɛ, Monnkɔdom asaase bio ara da wɔ nyimpa ntsi, osiandɛ minyim dɛ nyimpa n’akoma mu nsusui muo dze fitsi no mbofra mu; nkyii so mennkɛsɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu biara bio ara da, dɛ mbrɛ mayɛ yi.
translated: When the LORD smelled the sacrifice, the LORD said in his heart, "Never again will I curse the ground because of humans, even though every inclination of the human heart is evil from childhood. And never again will I destroy all living creatures as I have done."

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Na hom ho suro nye hom ho hu mbra asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara do; dza wɔwea daadze nyinara, na po mu nam nyinara, wɔdze ahyɛ hom nsa.
translated: Do not be afraid or terrified of anything on the earth, all creeping things and all birds of the air; everything that moves on the ground, and all the fish in the sea, I have given into your hands.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Na nam a no bɔgyaa da ho wɔ no mu dze, mma hom nndzi.
translated: And the meat that was left over in it, do not eat.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa, nye ne mba a wɔka no ho no dɛ,
translated: And God spoke to Noah and his children who were with him, saying,

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ medze mununkum meba asaase do a, wobohu nyankontɔn no wɔ mununkum no mu,
translated: And it shall come to pass, that when I bring clouds over the earth, the rainbow will be seen in the clouds,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: na mebɛkaa m’ahyɛmu a ɔda emi nye hom nye honam nyinara mu abɔdze a nkwa wɔ mu nyinara ntamu; na nsu no nnkɛyɛ nsuyirii bio asɛɛ honam nyinara.
translated: And I will establish my covenant with you and with all living creatures; never again will the waters become a flood to destroy all flesh.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Na Noa no wenyin no fii n’enyi do, na ɔtsee dza ne ba kuma no dze ayɛ no.
translated: And Noa’s tears welled up in his eyes when he heard what his youngest child had done to him.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: na Nahor n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.
translated: Nahor lived two hundred years after that, and he had sons and daughters.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ Abram, na ɔsee dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi bɛma hɔn: na hɔ na Abram sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE a oyii noho kyerɛɛ no no.
translated: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, "I will give this land to your descendants." So Abram built an altar there to the LORD who had appeared to him.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Na Farao hyɛɛ nkorɔfo ma wokegyaa ɔno, nye ne yer, nye dza ɔwɔ nyinara wɔ ɔkwan do.
translated: And Pharaoh commanded the people to let him, his wife, and all that he had go on the way.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Na Abram nyaa no ho kɛsenara wɔ fiembowa, nye dwetɛ, nye sika mu.
translated: Abram became very rich in livestock, silver, and gold.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Na otuu no kwan fi Pomu afamu araa kodur Bɛtɛl, beebi mbrɛ ahyɛse no n’asesee wɔ mu no, wɔ Bɛtɛl nye Ai ntamu:
translated: And he went on his way from the edge of the sea as far as Bethel, to the place where he had first set up his tent, between Bethel and Ai:

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Na Abram see Lot dɛ, Meserɛ wo, mma emma aperper mmba emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mo mbowahwɛfo nye wo mbowahwɛfo ntamu; osiandɛ yɛyɛ enuanom.
translated: And Abram said to Lot, Please, let there be no quarrel between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are brothers.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Nna Lot faa Jordan Tɛtɛrmu nyinara; na Lot tui faa boka afamu: na ɔbaako paa noho fii ne nyɛnko ho.
translated: Then Lot crossed the whole Jordan plain; and Lot journeyed towards the east: and he separated himself from his companion.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram tsenaa Keenan asaase do, na Lot tsenaa Tɛtɛrmu kurow no mu, na ɔdze n’asesee tutui araa dze kodur Sodom.
translated: Abram lived in the land of Canaan, and Lot lived among the cities of the plain, pitching his tents near Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Afei Sodomfo yɛ ehuhufo nye ndzebɔnyɛfo kɛsenara tsia EWURADZE.
translated: Now the people of Sodom are wicked and sinners exceedingly against the LORD.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Na mebɛma w’asefo ayɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma: na sɛ obi tum kan asaase do huntuma a, ɔnodze nna wobotum akan w’asefo no.
translated: And I will make your descendants as plentiful as the dust of the earth: so that if anyone can count the dust of the earth, then your descendants can also be counted.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a otsia duanan no mu na Kedorlaomer na ahemfo a wɔka no ho no bae, na wobokum Refafo wɔ Asterot-karnaim, nye Zuzfo wɔ Ham, nye Emfo wɔ Savɛ-kiriataim,
translated: And in the fourteenth year, Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and struck down the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, and the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: na Horfo wɔ hɔn bepɔw Seir do, dze kodu El-paran a ɔwɔ sar no ho no.
translated: And Horites were in their hill country of Seir, as far as El-paran which is by the wilderness.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Na wɔfaa Lot, Abram no nua ba a ɔtse Sodom no, nye ne ndzɛmba, na wosii mu kɔree.
translated: And they took Lot, Abram's nephew who lived in Sodom, and his possessions, and departed.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: gyedɛ dza mberantsɛ no edzi no nko, na Aner, Eskol nye Mamre a wɔnye me kɔree no hɔn kyɛpɛn.
translated: except what the young men have eaten, and the share belonging to the men Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre who went with me.

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Na Abram see dɛ, EWURADZE Nyankopɔn, ebɛnadze na edze bɛma me, hwɛ dɛ minnyi ba na menam, na nyia obedzi m’adze nye Eliezer a ofi Damaskɔs anaa?
translated: But Abram said, LORD God, what will you give me, since I am childless and the one who will inherit my estate is Eliezer of Damascus?

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE a medze wo fi Kaldeafo Ur wɔ Kaldea bae, dɛ medze asaase yi rebɛma wo, ma edze aayɛ egyapadze.
translated: And he said to him, I am the LORD who brought you from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess as an inheritance.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Na Sarai see Abram dɛ, Afei hwɛ, EWURADZE esiw me awoo kwan; meserɛ wo, kɔ m’afonabaa nkyɛn; annyɛ a mibenya no ho mba. Na Abram tsiee Sarai ne ndze.
translated: Now Sarai said to Abram, Look, the LORD has kept me from having children; I beg you, go to my maidservant; perhaps I will obtain children by her. And Abram listened to Sarai’s voice.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Na Abram tsenaa Keenan asaase do mfe du n’ekyir no, Abram ne yer Sarai faa n’afonabaa Hagar, Egyptnyi no, na ɔdze no maa no kun Abram awar.
translated: Abram lived in the land of Canaan for ten years, and after that, Abram and his wife Sarai took her maidservant Hagar, the Egyptian, and gave her to her husband Abram as a wife.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Na ɔkɔr Hagar nkyɛn, na onyinsenii: na aber a ohun dɛ onyinsen no, obuu no wuraba enyimtsia.
translated: So he went to Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked down on her mistress.

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Na Abram see Sarai dɛ, Hwɛ, w’afonabaa wɔ wo nsamu, yɛ no dɛ mbrɛ woara ehwɛ a oye no ara. Na Sarai nye no dzii no dzendzenndzen, ma ofii n’enyim guanee.
translated: And Abram said to Sarai, "Look, your maidservant is in your hands; do to her as you please." And Sarai dealt harshly with her, so she fled from her presence.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe hun no wɔ nsunyiwa bi ho wɔ sar do, Sur kwan mu nsunyiwa no ho a.
translated: And the angel of the LORD appeared to him by a well of water in the wilderness, by the well on the way to Shur.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe no see no dɛ, San kɔ wo wuraba hɔ, na kɛbrɛ woho adze wɔ n’ase.
translated: And the angel of the LORD said to him, Return to your mistress, and humble yourself under her hand.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Na mebɛma m’ahyɛmu ada emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mebɛma adɔr mbordo.
translated: I will make my covenant between me and you, and I will multiply you greatly.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Na mebɛma awo mbordo, na mayɛ wo amanaman, na ahemfo efi wo mu epue.
translated: I will make you fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings will come from you.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Na ɔnye no gyaa kasa no, Nyankopɔn fii Abraham nkyɛn kɔr sor.
translated: And as soon as he finished speaking with him, God went up from Abraham.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Mo wura, sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, meserɛ wo, mma mmpa w’akowaa ho nnkɔ.
translated: And he said, My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, I beg you, do not pass by your servant.

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mesi mo bo dɛ mebɛkasa akyerɛ EWURADZE: egyangyanbiara eduonu na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ: Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian eduonu no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.
translated: And he said, Now look, I tell you that I will speak to the LORD: there are twenty elders you will find there: And he said, Because of the twenty, I will not destroy it.

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ao mma mma EWURADZE no bo mmfuw, na menkasa prɛko pɛ yi ara: egyangyanbiara du na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ; Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian du no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.
translated: And he said, Oh children, do not anger the LORD, and let me say just this: there are ten lepers you see there; And he said, Because of the ten, I did not reject them.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Afei hwɛ, mowɔ mbabaa beenu a wonnhun banyin: meserɛ hom, wɔmma minyi hɔn mbrɛ hom, na dza homara hwɛ a oye no ara hom nyɛ hɔn: mbarimba yi dze mma wɔnnyɛ hɔn biribiara; susuampa dɛ wɔaba me hatsɛw no nwin ase yi.
translated: Now look, you have two girls who have not known a man: I beg you, let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; but do nothing to these men, for they have come under the protection of my roof.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Na aber a Lot dur Zoar no, nna ewia epue asaase do.
translated: But when Lot arrived at Zoar, the sun had risen over the land.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Na Abraham soɛɛr anapaatu kɔr mbrɛ ogyinae wɔ EWURADZE n’enyim no:
translated: And Abraham got up early in the morning and stood before the LORD:

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Na abakan no see ekumaba no dɛ, Hɛn egya ayɛ akɔdaa, na ɔbanyin biara nnyi asaase do a ɔbɛba hɛn nkyɛn dɛ mbrɛ asaase nyinara yɛ no:
translated: But the elders said about the coming death that, Our father has become a child, and there is no man on earth who will come to us forever, for the whole earth belongs to him.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see no adaaso mu, dɛ, ɛhɛɛ, minyim dɛ w’akoma mu tsewee mu na ayɛ yi, na mo so musuoo wo mu amma aannyɛ bɔn enntsia me: dɛm ntsi na mamma annka no.
translated: And God said in his abundance, look, I know that there is bitterness in your heart and it has become like this, and you did not let the sorrow in you lead you to sin against me: that is why I did not mention it.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Na Abimelek bisaa Abraham dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ihunii, ma ayɛ dɛm adze yi?
translated: And Abimelech asked Abraham, What did you see, that made you do this thing?

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Nyankopɔn maa mifii m’egya fie mikekyinii no, nna mesee ɔbaa no dɛ, Ayamuyie a fa yɛ me nye yi; beebiara mbrɛ yebodu no, ka no wɔ mo ho dɛ, Mo nua a.
translated: And the woman said, "The favor that God gave me when I left my father's house and wandered, tell this woman: This is my companion in suffering; wherever you go, say it about her, My sister."

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Osiandɛ nna EWURADZE esiw Abimelek ne fie yafun nyinara mu kwan petsee wɔ Abraham ne yer Sara ntsi.
translated: Because the LORD had completely closed every womb in Abimelek's household on account of Abraham's wife Sarah.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Na abofra no nyinii, na wotwaa no enumfo: na da a wotwaa Isaak enumfo no, Abraham too pon kɛsenara.
translated: And the child grew, and was weaned: and the day Isaac was weaned, Abraham held a great feast.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Soɛr, kɛma abofra no do, na fa wo nsa suo no mu; na mebɛyɛ no ɔman kɛse.
translated: Be patient, love the child, and hold them in your arms; and I will make them a great nation.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Na Nyankopɔn buee n’enyiwa, na ohun nsubura bi; na ɔkɔree, na ɔkɛhyɛɛ bɔdambɔ no nsu ma, na ɔmaa abofra no nsu ma ɔnomee.
translated: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went, and filled the bottle with water, and gave the child drink.

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
text: Na ɔtsenaa Paran sar do; na ne na kɔwaar yer wɔ Egypt asaase do brɛɛ no. Abraham Nye Abimelek Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: He lived in the land of Paran; and his mother found him a wife in the land of Egypt. Abraham Makes a Covenant with Abimelech

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber no no mu na Abimelek nye ne safohen tsitsir Fikol kasaa kyerɛɛ Abraham dɛ, Nyankopɔn dzi w’ekyir wɔ dza eyɛ nyinara mu:
translated: Now it was at that time that Abimelech and his army commander Phicol spoke to Abraham, saying, God is with you in everything you do:

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Na Abimelek see dɛ, Minnyim nyia ɔyɛɛ iyi: na wo so annka annkyerɛ me, na nkyii so menntsee pɛn dɛ ndɛ.
translated: Then Abimelech said, I did not know who did this: and you also did not tell me, and I did not hear of it until today.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Na ne ndaansa no Abraham maa n’enyiwa do, na ohun bea no wɔ ekyir noho ara.
translated: And the day before, Abraham looked up, and the woman was still behind him.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Na Abraham see dɛ, Me ba, Nyankopɔn noara bɛkyerɛ eguambaa a wɔdze no bɔbɔ ɔhyew afɔr: na hɔn beenu nyina bɔɔ mu kɔree.
translated: And Abraham said, My son, God himself will provide the lamb for a burnt offering: and the two of them went on together.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Na Abraham tseen ne nsa, na ɔfaa sekan no dɛ ɔdze ruboku ne ba no.
translated: And Abraham stretched out his hand, and took the knife to slay his son.

ref: “But sir,” the man replied, “the property is worth only
text: Mo wura, tsie me: dwetɛ dwetɛbona ahaanan asaase nye dɛn wɔ emi na ɔwo ntamu? Dɛm ntsi sie wo fun.
translated: My master, listen to me: what is four hundred ounces of silver and land to me compared to you? That is why I keep you in my heart.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Na Abraham see, ne fie somfo panyin a ɔhwɛ adze a ɔwɔ nyinara do no, dɛ, Meserɛ wo, fa wo nsa to m’aserɛ ase:
translated: And Abraham said to the chief servant of his household, who was in charge of all he had, Please, put your hand under my thigh:

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Na sɛ ɔbaa no ammpɛ dɛ ɔbɛka wo do a, ɔno na wo ho efi me ntam yi mu: me ba no ara na mma mmfa no nnkɔ hɔ bio.
translated: And if the woman does not want to love you, she is the one who is no longer part of this relationship: my own child should not go there again.

ref: While he was still praying, a beautiful unmarried young woman came by with a water jar on her shoulder. She was Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his wife Milcah. Rebekah walked past Abraham's servant, then went over to the well, and filled her water jar. When she started back,
text: Na akataasia no no ho yɛ fɛw dodow, na ɔyɛ ɔbaabun, osiandɛ ɔbanyin biara nnhun no pɛn: na osianee kɔr bura no mu, na ɔkɛhyɛɛ n’ehina ma, na ɔbaa sor.
translated: The girl was very beautiful, and she was a virgin, for no man had ever known her: and she went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came up.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Na wɔdze edziban besii n’enyim dɛ ondzi: na ɔsee dɛ, Minnkedzi, gyedɛ mabɔ m’amandzɛɛ ana. Na ɔsee dɛ, Bɔ ɛ.
translated: And they brought food before him to eat: but he said, I will not eat, unless I have told my message first. And he said, Say it.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Kɔ m’egya fie, nye m’ebusua mu, na kɛgye yer ma me ba.
translated: Go to my father's house, among my family, and find a wife for my child.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: hwɛ migyina nsubura yi ho; na ma ɔmbra dɛ akataasia a obopue abɛsaw bi a mebɛse no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma me w’ehina mu nsu kakra ma monnom;
translated: Look, I am standing by this well; let her come out to draw water, and I will say to her, Please, give me a little water from your jar to drink;

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: na ɔbɛse me dɛ, ɔwoara nom bi, na mebɛsaw bi ama w’efumpɔnkɔ so no: ɔnoara na ma ɔnyɛ ɔbaa a EWURADZE eyi no ama mo wura ne ba.
translated: And he said to me, "You yourself drink some, and I will pour some on your horse: she is the one whom the LORD has chosen to be your master's son's wife."

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Na mibisaa no dɛ, Woana babaa nye wo? Na ɔsee dɛ, Nahor ba Betuel a Milka woo no maa no no, ne babaa nye me: na medze mpɛtsea no hyɛɛ no hwen, na medze nsakɔnadze no hyehyɛɛ ne nsa.
translated: So I asked her, Whose daughter are you? And she said, I am the daughter of Bethuel son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to him: then I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her arms.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Na wodzidzii na wɔnomee, ɔno nye mbanyin a wɔka no ho no, na wotsintsiim hɔ anafua no nyinara. Na wɔsoɛɛr anapa no, ɔsee dɛ, Hom ngya me kwan ma monkɔ mo wura hɔ.
translated: They ate and drank, he and the men with him, and they spent the whole night there. And in the morning, he said, Please let me go so I can return to my master.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Aman ebien na wɔwɔ wo yamu, Na nkorɔfo ahorow ebien na wɔbɛpaa hɔnho mu efi wo yafun mu; Na ɔman no mu kor bɛyɛ dzen asen kor; Na ɔpanyin no bɔsom ekumaba no.
translated: And the LORD said, Some nations are within you, and different peoples will come out from your womb; and one nation will be stronger than the other; and the elder will serve the younger.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Na Esau see Jakob dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma me edziban yi bi na mindzi; na matɔ bitsi: dɛm ntsi wɔdze too no dzin Edom.
translated: And Esau said to Jacob, Please, let me eat some of this food, for I am hungry; that is why he was called Edom.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ no, na ɔsee dɛ, Mma nnkɔ Egypt; tsena asaase a mebɛkyerɛ wo no do.
translated: And the LORD appeared to him and said, Do not go down to Egypt; stay in the land that I will show you.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Na Isaak tsenaa Gerar.
translated: And Isaac lived in Gerar.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”

translated: And Isaac asked them, why have you come here, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Na ɔfrɛɛ no Siba: dɛm ntsi kurow no ne dzin dze Beer-seba ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi.
translated: He called it Siba: that is why the name of the town is Beersheba to this day.

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.

translated: And he said, Now look, I have become a child, I do not know your death.

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: na yɛ me edziban a ɔyɛ huam, dɛ mbrɛ mepɛ, na fa brɛ me, ma mindzi; na me kra nhyira wo ana mewu.
translated: If there is food that is fragrant, when I want it, bring it to me, let me eat; and my soul will bless you before I die.

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Na Jakob see ne na Rebeka dɛ, Hwɛ, mo nua Esau no ho wɔ nhwi, na emi mo ho yɛ monomonom.
translated: And Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, Look, my brother Esau is hairy, but I have smooth skin.

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na Jakob see n’egya dɛ, Emi nye w’abakan Esau; mayɛ dɛ mbrɛ ekyerɛɛ me no: soɛr meserɛ wo, tsena hɔ na dzi me hanam yi bi, ama wo kra eehyira me.
translated: And Jacob said to his father, I am your firstborn Esau; I have done as you told me: please, sit and eat some of my game, so that your soul may bless me.

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Na Isaak bisaa ne ba no dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ma wo nsa kaa no ntsɛm dɛm, me ba? Na obuaa dɛ, Osiandɛ EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn maa mihyiaa yie.
translated: Then Isaac asked his son, How did you find it so quickly, my son? And he answered, Because the LORD your God gave me success.

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Fa pin me nkyɛn, ma mindzi me ba ne hanam yi bi, ma me kra nhyira wo. Na ɔdze piin ne nkyɛn, ma odzii: na ɔdze nsa brɛɛ no, ma ɔnomee.
translated: And he said, Bring me some bread, so that I may eat my child's flesh, so that my soul may bless you. And he brought it to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Aman nsom wo, Na amanaman nkotow wo: Yɛ wo nuanom do wura, Na wo na mba nkotow wo: Ndom nka hɔn a wɔdom wo nyinara, Na nhyira nka hɔn a wohyira wo nyinara. Esau Bisa Nhyira
translated: The nations serve you, and peoples bow down to you: You are the lord over your brothers, and your mother’s sons bow down to you: May those who bless you be blessed, and those who curse you be cursed. Esau Seeks Blessing

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Ana ɔno wɔmmfrɛ no Jakob bi? Iyi ka ho a oedzi me emim mpɛn ebien: ɔagye me panyin; na, hwɛ, afei ɔagye me nhyira. Na obisaa dɛ, Ana enngyaa me nhyira biara?
translated: And Esau said, "Isn't he rightly named Jacob? For he has cheated me these two times: he took my birthright; and now he has taken my blessing." And he asked, "Haven't you reserved any blessing for me?"

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Na Isaak frɛɛ Jakob, na ohyiraa no, na ɔhyɛɛ no, na ɔsee no dɛ, Mma nnwar yer wɔ Keenan mbabaa mu.
translated: Isaac called Jacob, blessed him, and commanded him, saying, Do not marry a Canaanite woman.

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.

translated: But Jacob obeyed his father and his mother, and he went to Paddan-aram.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Na, hwɛ, EWURADZE gyina n’etsifi, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE, w’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na Isaak ne Nyankopɔn: asaase a eda do no, medze mebɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo.
translated: Now, look, the LORD stood before him and said, I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac; the land on which you are lying, I will give to you and your descendants.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Na w’asefo bɛyɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma, na ebɛtserɛw akɔ anee, boka, nye kwaamu, nye pomu: na ɔwo nye w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase do ebusua ebusua nyinara.
translated: And your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread out to the west, east, north, and south; and through you and your descendants all the families of the earth will be blessed.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Na Jakob bɔɔ anohoba see dɛ, Sɛ Nyankopɔn dzi m’ekyir, na ɔhwɛ mo do wɔ ɔkwan yi a murutu yi mu, na ɔma me edziban ma midzi, nye atar ma mehyɛ,
translated: And Jacob made a vow saying, If God will be with me, and watch over me on this journey I am taking, and give me food to eat and clothes to wear,

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.

translated: And Jacob asked them, My brothers, where are you from? And they answered, We are from Haran.

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”

translated: And he asked them, Is he well? And they said, He is well: and, look, his daughter Rachel is coming with the sheep.

translated: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's relative and that he was Rebekah's son; and Rachel ran and told her father.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.

translated: But in the morning, he took his daughter Leah and brought her to him; and he slept with her.

text: Na onyinseen bio, na ɔwoo babanyin; na ɔsee dɛ, Afei na mo kun nye me bɛka, osiandɛ mawo mbabanyin baasa ama no: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Levi asekyerɛ nye; Ahofam.
translated: And she conceived again, and bore a son; and she said, Now this time my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons: therefore his name was called Levi, meaning; Attachment.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Na onyinseen bio, na ɔwoo babanyin: na ɔsee dɛ, Afei mibeyi EWURADZE ayɛw: dɛm ntsi ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Juda; a asekyerɛ nye; Ayɛyi na ogyaa awoo.
translated: And she conceived again, and bore a son: and she said, Now I will praise the LORD: therefore she called his name Judah; and the meaning is; Praise is what she gave at birth.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,

translated: When Leah saw that, a son has come, she named him Gad which means, Fortune.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Na Leah see dɛ, Ahomkanyi nye me, na mbabaa bɛfrɛ me ɔhomkanyi: na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Aser a asekyerɛ nye Nhyira.
translated: When Leah saw that, she said, "I am blessed, and women will call me blessed." So she named him Asher, which means Blessing.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,

translated: And during the wheat harvest, Reuben went out and found some mandrakes, and he brought them to his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, Please give me some of your son's mandrakes.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Na Jakob fi hamu ewimbir reba no, Leah puei kehyiaa no kwan, na ɔsee dɛ, Bra me nkyɛn, na ampaara medze me ba ne mandreke abɔ wo paa. Na ɔnye no daa anafua no.
translated: When Jacob was returning from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, "Come to me, for I have surely hired you with my son's mandrakes." So he spent the night with her.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin: na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn eyi m’ahorohoraa:
translated: And the woman conceived, and bore a son: and she said, God has taken away my reproach.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.

translated: And it happened that when Rachel had given birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Allow me to go so that I may return to my own homeland.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Ama daakye eba m’apaabɔdze a ɔda w’enyim yi do a, me ndzeyɛtsenenee eebua ama me; mpɔnkye no mu dza hɔn ho nnyɛ woranworan nye ntokowa ntokowa no, na nguantsen no hɔn mu dza wɔnnyɛ etuntum no nyinara, ɔno na sɛ wohu wɔ mo hɔ a, wombu no adzewiadze.
translated: Ama, in the future when my good deeds stand before you, my righteousness will answer for me; the goats that are not spotted or speckled, and all the sheep that are not black among them, if you find any such with me, consider them as stolen.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Na Jakob paa nguambaa no na ɔmaa nguan no hɔn enyim kɔr Laban nguan no mu nsensanee nsensanee nye etuntum no nyinara hɔn do; na ɔyɛɛ narankasa no nguankuw wɔ nkyɛn, na ɔammfa ammfora Laban no nguan no mu.
translated: Now Jacob separated the lambs and made the flocks face toward the streaked and all the dark-colored animals among Laban’s flocks; and he put his own herds apart and did not put them with Laban’s animals.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Na homara nyim dɛ m’ahoɔdzen nyinara na medze asom hom egya.
translated: And you should know that all my strength I have used to serve you, father.

text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the Bethel God, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me; now arise, leave this land, and return to the land of your birth.

translated: And he took all his livestock, and all the wealth he had gained in Paddan-aram, and set out to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.

text: Na Jakob wiaa noho guanee; osiandɛ ɔannka annkyerɛ Laban, Sirianyi no.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara guanee; na ɔsoɛree, na otwaa Esutsen no, na ɔmaa n’enyi kɔr Gilead bepɔw do. Laban Tsie Jakob
translated: He took everything he owned and set out; he got up, crossed the river, and headed toward the hill country of Gilead. Laban pursues Jacob.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Na ɔfaa no nkorɔfo kaa noho, na otsiiw no nda esuon kwan; na ɔbɔtoo no Gilead bepɔw do.
translated: And he took the people and led them by the way of the wilderness; and he camped on Mount Gilead.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Na Laban bisaa Jakob dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi, na ewia woho eguan me, na asoa me mbabaa dze hɔn rokɔ dɛ hɔn a wɔdze mfowa afa hɔn ndɔmmum yi?
translated: Then Laban asked Jacob, What have you done, that you have deceived me and carried away my daughters like captives taken in war?

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob was angry, and he argued with Laban: and Jacob answered Laban, saying, What is my offence? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me like this?

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac had not been with me, surely by now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my hardship and the labor of my hands, and last night he rebuked you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,

translated: I have learned about cattle, donkeys, sheep, goats, and lambs: and I have sent them to tell your master, so that I may find favor in your sight.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Na abɔfo no sanee baa Jakob hɔ, dɛ, Yɛkɔr wo nua Esau hɔ na mbom ɔnoara so reba ebehyia wo kwan, na mbarimba ahaanan ka no ho.
translated: Then the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We went to your brother Esau, and he is also coming to meet you, and four hundred men are with him.

translated: And he said, If Esau comes to one group and attacks it, then the group that is left will escape.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Jakob see dɛ, Ao, m’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na m’egya Isaak ne Nyankopɔn, ao, EWURADZE a esee me dɛ, San kɔ wo man mu, nye w’ebusua mu, na mebɛyɛ wo yie:
translated: And Jacob said, Oh, the God of my father Abraham, and the God of my father Isaac, oh, LORD who has taken care of me, Return to your country, and to your family, and I will prosper you:

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza odzi kan no dɛ, Sɛ mo nua Esau nye wo hyia, na obisa wo dɛ, Woana n’abofra nye wo, erokɔ henfa, na dza wodzi w’enyim yi woana dze a?
translated: And he instructed him further, If your brother Esau meets you and asks you, Whose child are you, where are you going, and whose are these ahead of you?

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ɔno se dɛ, W’akowaa Jakob dze a; ɔyɛ akyɛdze a wɔdze rekɛma mo wura Esau: na, hwɛ, ɔnoara so dzi hɛn ekyir.
translated: He said, “It is your servant Jacob’s; it is a gift he is sending to my lord Esau. And look, he himself is behind us.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, You will no longer be called Jacob, but rather, Israel: because you have struggled with God and with humans, and have prevailed.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe behyiaa no.
translated: When Jacob was on his way, God's angels met him.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: When Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp." So he named that place Mahanaim.

translated: Do not harm cattle, donkeys, sheep, goats, or lambs and young animals: for I have sent them to tell your master, so that I may find favor before you.
